title: Draft Improved README Structure and Content
description: Create a new, comprehensive README draft that addresses all gaps and improvements identified in the audit.
details: Structure the README to include: project overview, quickstart instructions, monorepo layout, development workflow, code quality tools (Husky, ESLint, Prettier), infrastructure provisioning (Terraform, Cloudflare D1), AI command usage, troubleshooting, and contribution guidelines. Use clear, concise language and provide code snippets or command examples where helpful.
test_strategy: Peer review: Share the draft with a team member or new contributor to ensure clarity and completeness. Confirm all PRD requirements are addressed.

title: Update and Format README in Repository
description: Replace the existing README.md with the improved version, ensuring proper Markdown formatting and readability.
details: Copy the drafted content into README.md at the project root. Use Markdown best practices: headings, lists, code blocks, and links. Add badges (build, license, etc.) if relevant. Check for formatting consistency and accessibility (e.g., alt text for images).
test_strategy: Preview the README in GitHub or VS Code to verify formatting. Check for broken links or formatting errors.

title: Validate README with New Contributor
description: Test the effectiveness of the new README by having a new or uninvolved contributor follow its instructions from scratch.
details: Ask a team member unfamiliar with the project to follow the README from clone to running the dev environment. Collect feedback on clarity, missing steps, and overall onboarding experience. Note any issues or confusion encountered.
test_strategy: Survey or interview the tester. Confirm they can set up and run the project in under 5 minutes as per PRD goals.

title: Incorporate Feedback and Finalize README
description: Refine the README based on feedback from the validation step, making final edits for clarity, completeness, and accuracy.
details: Address all actionable feedback from the new contributor test. Clarify confusing sections, add missing details, and fix any errors. Commit the finalized README to the repository.
test_strategy: Final review: Ensure all feedback is addressed and README meets PRD standards. Optionally, repeat the validation with another new user.

title: Audit Existing README Content
description: Review the current README file to identify missing, outdated, or unclear sections based on the PRD and project scope.
details: Open the current README.md at the root of the repository. Compare its contents against the PRD, ensuring all in-scope features, setup instructions, usage guides, and developer workflows are covered. Note any areas that are incomplete, inaccurate, or could be improved for clarity and onboarding speed.
test_strategy: Checklist: Ensure all PRD features and workflows are represented. Confirm that setup, development, and deployment steps are present and accurate.
